links.targetTitle: DocumentToolbar exposes className enabledControls and callback props
Revision History panel styling requires scoped CSS on document-editor-context since no props are exposed

title: Document state is organized into global and local scopes for sync control
content:
Document state in Powerhouse is organized into scopes:

- **Global scope** (`state.global`): Synchronized data that lives on a Reactor or remote server. This is where your document's main content belongs. Most use cases work exclusively with `state.global`.
- **Local scope** (`state.local`): For edge cases where data should only exist on the local device and not sync.

A future **authorization scope** is planned for permission-controlled data.

When accessing document state in editors, you use `selectedDocument.state.global.items` (for example). Operations are also scoped - `document.operations.global` contains the operation history for the global scope.
description: Global scope (state.global) syncs to Reactors; local scope (state.local) stays on the local device only.
